subject: Project Management
title: The Iron Triangle
description: A fast breakdown of The Iron Triangle for builders.

hook.text: Every project balances three constraints: scope, time, and cost. Change one, the others must shift.

buildup.text: Want more features? It'll cost more or take longer. Want it faster? Cut scope or increase budget.

discovery.text: The 'Iron Triangle' (or 'Triple Constraint') is PM's oldest and most fundamental law.

twist.text: Clients often demand all three fixed: full scope, on time, under budget. Physics says no.

climax.text: Good project managers negotiate the triangle openly. Bad ones pretend it doesn't exist.

punchline.text: Scope, time, cost. Pick two. That's the deal.

quiz.question: What is the Iron Triangle in project management?
options: The tradeoff between scope, time, and cost
